info.title: CollisionSpike EVA Validation
info.description: Custom connector over the EVA validation Azure Function — the ONE shared implementation of the EVA readiness contract so the Power Automate status machine and the Code App computeReadiness() agree byte-for-byte (Phase-1 §5.4 drift mitigation). Pure domain logic ported from the canonical TS image-rules.ts + case-status.ts; NO gate (always on), NO secrets, NO upstream call. Returns { fieldsValid, imagesValid, openIssues[] }.

post.summary: Validate EVA readiness for a Case
post.description: Returns { fieldsValid, imagesValid, openIssues[] }. PREFERRED shape is body-in: pass { case, evidence } so the Function stays stateless and the flow remains the single Dataverse reader. The caseId-only shape is accepted for compatibility with the current status-evaluate flow but returns a safe-negative + an advisory (the Function has no Dataverse identity by design).

ValidateCaseRequest.description: Provide EITHER { case (+ evidence) } (preferred, body-in) OR { caseId } (compat).

caseId.description: Compat shape (current status-evaluate). Returns a safe-negative + advisory because the Function is stateless by design.

case.description: The Case's 12 EVA fields. Accepts contract keys (work_provider, ...), Dataverse columns (cr1bd_eva*), or an embedded {value,reviewState} per field. Optional 'reviewStates' map (contract-key -> int) drives the open-issue check.

evidence.description: The Case's Evidence rows. Accepts image-rule contract keys or Dataverse cr1bd_* columns (kind, imageRole, registrationVisible, acceptedForEva, excluded).

fieldsValid.description: True when every required EVA field is non-empty.

imagesValid.description: True when >=2 accepted images incl. >=1 overview (registration visible) + >=1 damage_closeup.

openIssues.description: Every readiness gap (missing fields, image-rule failures, fields needing review).

apiKeyHeader.name: x-functions-key
apiKeyHeader.description: Azure Functions host/function key. Stored as a connection secret in Power Platform, never in this spec. No other secrets — this Function holds none.
